issue_title: Conservative content import
issue_labels: [{'id': 492822760, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/DEV:%20backend', 'name': 'DEV: backend', 'color': 'DFDFDF', 'default': False}, {'id': 667034823, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/P1%20-%20important', 'name': 'P1 - important', 'color': 'ff8400', 'default': False}, {'id': 492818262, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/learningequality/kolibri/labels/TASK:%20new%20feature', 'name': 'TASK: new feature', 'color': '72486f', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### Context

When we introducing new content types, we want to make sure Kolibri installations will not import content types they do not support (i.e. have plugins/renderers).

Currently Kolibri will import content nodes even if it doesn't have the renderer for that content type.


### Change needed

Kolibri import screens and views need to be "conservative" and show only content nodes and files it can render.

Note a supported content kind (Document) could have unsupported files, e.g. new document nodes can have either PDF or ePub files.

issue_title: nidcpower.Session.set_sequence() can crash you
issue_body:
Method:

    set_sequence(self, values, source_delays=None)

corresponds to function:

    ViStatus _VI_FUNC niDCPower_SetSequence(
        ViSession vi,
        ViConstString channelName,
        const ViReal64 values[],
        const ViReal64 sourceDelays[],
        ViUInt32 size);
 
The way the function works, sourceDelays array is an optional parameter. If you pass NULL (in Python the default is None), then the driver uses whatever default for sourceDelay it has. This doesn't work in our generated code:

    TypeError: object of type 'NoneType' has no len()

So the default doesn't work. That's bad. It gets worse...

Suppose the Python client does pass two lists, but they are of different size. The generated code will grab the size from one of the two, and pass that to the Library. If the one it grabbed it from happens to be larger, then you will end up passing a bunch of garbage from the smaller one (or perhaps zeroes, if ctypes zeroes out array element on allocation).

There are a few system tests, but it's hard to verify that all the right values make it down correctly.

* test_set_sequence_default_source_delays()
* test_set_sequence_no_source_delays()

issue_body:
Some method names in the documentation (docstring, readthedocs) shows C function names instead of the Python names.

In reality, this happens due to the function names being misspelled, so our code-generator doesn't replace them properly.

We can hack something to "fix" up the names, or we can get NI to fix the source of the problem (NI Internal bug 699079).

Examples:

*   nifgen_AllocateWaveform
*   nifgen_CreateWaveformF64
*   nifgen_CreateWaveformI16
*   nifgen_CreateWaveformFromFileI16
*   nifgen_CreateWaveformFromFileF64
*   nifgen_CreateWaveformFromFileHWS

issue_title: previous_version and next_version: unintuitive behaviour for related objects
issue_body:
How to reproduce.

1. Create a Versionable object having relation (m2m or o2m) with another Versionable. Save this first version.
2. Create a new version of this model.
3. Using the latest version call on it the previous_version() method to get back the initially created version.
4. Navigate the relation of that origin version and see that you don't get anything back as you would expect.

Possible solution:
Add an optional as_of parameter to previous_version() and next_version(), so that the object returned will propagate this given as_of time to the queries generated for related objects.  If no as_of time is given, set the object's query_time to it's version_end_date minus 1 microsecond (or to None if it's the current object)

In the case that the given as_of lies outside of the version's validity period, an Exception will be raised.

However:
How common is the use case that we ask for a previous version and know it's expected validity period?  Perhaps previous_version / next_version should not have an additional as_of parameter - they should just set the returned object's query_time to it's version_end_date minus 1 microsecond (or to None if it's the current object).  The caller can then adjust the object's as_of time themselves, if needed.

issue_title: loaders.Web streamable in Python 2
issue_body:
#### Python 3

`loaders.Web` returns streamable and resetable file-like object because `urlopen()` returns true file-like object as an http response. 
#### Python 2

`loaders.Web` returns resetable file-like object loaded to a memory because `urlopen()` returns not a true file-like object as a http response (`response.read()` is used). Some wrapper is needed.

https://github.com/okfn/tabulator-py/blob/master/tabulator/loaders/web.py

issue_title: Proxy errors that aren't caused by a proxy
issue_body:
From a customer:

> conda wrongly reports that the proxy is not set when it can't reach a channel.
> 
> Reason of the problem is actually that the proxy expects requests to be routed to the internet. There is no route from the proxy to the lab's LAN. Therefore conda cannot reach the anaconda server within the lab’s LAN.
> 
> The workaround has been to add a NO_PROXY environment variable and exclude the lab's network domain. IP ranges does not seems to be accepted.
> 
> However it would have been easier to understand what the problem is if errors were reported more accurately

issue_title: BUG: conda 4.0.4 fails to correctly resolve build numbers
issue_body:
Somewhere between `conda-4.0.2` and `conda-4.0.4` a bug has slipped in the dependency resolving logic.

One of my packages depends on gnuplot indirectly via a second dependency. There are multiple builds available in the bioconda channel:

```
gnuplot-5.0.3-0
gnuplot-5.0.3-1
gnuplot-5.0.3-2
gnuplot-5.0.3-3
```

conda-4.0.2 correctly identifies `gnuplot-5.0.3-3` as the package to pull in. `>= conda-4.0.4`, conda erroneously thinks `gnuplot-5.0.3-0` is the correct dependency. 

`conda install gnuplot` correctly identifies `gnuplot-5.0.3-3` as the correct version for both versions.

issue_title: Colon in environment name results in misconfigured PATH
issue_body:
Installed a fresh version of Miniconda in `/home/ryan/Conda` and created a new environment with a colon (:) in its name. Activating my new environment and running Python results in the base Python installation being called (`/home/ryan/Conda/bin/python`) rather than Python installed in my new environment (`/home/ryan/Conda/envs/.../bin/python`), meaning that any installed Python packages are not importable. Perhaps Conda should not allow colons to be present in environment names? Or alternatively, this limitation should be made clear in documentation and a meaningful warning printed to the user when they try to do so. ## Current Behavior Environment names containing colons result in the PATH environment variable being incorrectly set upon activation. This is due to colons being used as a separator between paths in PATH. Escaping of colons in PATH is not possible, and therefore no path in PATH should contain a colon. ### Steps to Reproduce ``` $ conda create -n my:env python ``` Completes without error or suggestion that the user may want to reconsider their choice of environment name. All expected packages installed in `/home/ryan/Conda/envs/my:env`. ``` $ source activate my:env ``` Again, completes without complaining. Confirmed successfully working within my:env due to the (my:env) prefix on the prompt. ``` $ which python ``` Returns `/home/ryan/Conda/bin/python`, not the Python installed in the newly created environment which is located in `/home/ryan/Conda/envs/my:env/bin/`. ``` $ echo $PATH ``` Reveals the true problem; namely that this environment variable is set to `/home/ryan/Conda/envs/my:env/bin:/home/ryan/Conda/bin:...`. Notice the additional colon that separates what should be a single path `/home/ryan/Conda/envs/my:env/bin` into `/home/ryan/Conda/envs/my` and `env/bin`, which are illegitimate. ## Expected Behavior An error thrown to the user upon the attempted creation of an environment containing a colon in its name. The error should describe that such names are invalid. ## Environment Information

issue_title: ``python setup.py develop`` does not make a package available in preference to existing conda package
issue_body:
In https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1990, @msarahan recommends using ``python setup.py develop`` or ``pip install -e .`` instead of conda develop.

A problem I'm having, though, is that ``python setup.py develop`` does not override a package that's already installed by conda (and which might have been installed by conda while installing some other package, so I might not even be aware I already had it). If I ``conda remove`` the package so I can get my development version of the package, other packages I need might also get removed (if they depend on the package I'm removing, although I think I could ``conda remove --force`` a package to avoid that).

To further add to the confusion, several of us think the behavior used to be the other way around. That is, after ``python setup.py develop`` installing a package, it would forever be used in preference to the same package subsequently installed by conda. However, none of us is quite sure (we're all a bit confused).

What's the best way for developers to do something like ``python setup.py develop`` for a package that may already have been conda installed?
 
(I *think* ``pip install -e .`` works, in that it overrides a conda pacakge, but I'm not 100% sure as I have only just started using it and don't have enough practical experience of it to be confident it's working.)

Thanks

issue_title: put_template should be updating the index template
issue_body:
Method put template is defined as "Create an index template that will automatically be applied to new indices created"

In fact it updates the search template (_search/template) and not /_template/TEMPLATE_NAME

`    @query_params()
    def put_template(self, id, body, params=None):
        """
        Create a search template.
        `<http://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/search-template.html>`_

        :arg id: Template ID
        :arg body: The document
        """
        for param in (id, body):
            if param in SKIP_IN_PATH:
                raise ValueError("Empty value passed for a required argument.")
        return self.transport.perform_request('PUT', _make_path('_search',
            'template', id), params=params, body=body)`

issue_title: On-board Media Services
"az vm create" should do a pre-check for available SKUs
issue_body:
The default az vm create doesn't work for some location and will get error "The requested VM size 'Standard_DS1' is not available in this region". Unfortunately there is no easy way to find out the available region/sku combination from CLI. At this point user has to keep retrying different location, or open portal to figure out what's available.

I suggest we do similar pre-deployment check like portal: fetch the /providers/Microsoft.Compute/skus API and use that in auto-completion and validate/default a SKU to provision.

issue_title: [DNS] 'az network dns zone delete' should not support '--if-none-match'
issue_body:
The if-none-match HTTP header is only applicable on the Azure DNS REST API when creating a zone.

Therefore, 'az network dns zone delete' should not support '--if-none-match'.  (It makes no sense...it means 'delete this, but only if it doesn't exist already' !!)

Please remove this parameter.

issue_title: Feature request: a special "<text>" value for the hooks.yaml "files" field
issue_body:
Hi,

I had to implement this kind of "binary-files-filtering-out" today:
https://github.com/Lucas-C/pre-commit-hooks/blob/master/pre_commit_hooks/utils.py#L10

So I made a quick overview of the existing checkers, and I found only 2 that are targeting **any** text file: [end-of-file-fixer](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks/blob/master/hooks.yaml#L76) and [trailing-whitespaces](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks/blob/master/hooks.yaml#L106). Both use a long list of known text files prefixes.

If ever you think this use case could come in handy, I'd suggest to provide a magic value like `<any text file>` to the `files` parameter in _hooks.yaml_, so that hooks developers don't have to re-implement this filtering each time.

You can freely re-use the code I mentioned above if it comes handy to you.

Also, sorry for the 3-issues-opened-in-a-row :) I got really excited by this tool and suggestions for improvements just flowed in ! They are really just humble features suggestions.

Cheers

issue_title: How to interpret HMM states without labeling?
issue_body:
First of all, thanks for sharing the work on HMM, it's really usefull. 

My question is as follows. I have a data set which consists of positive floating-point numbers: `X = [x1, x2, ..., xn]`. Then I use this array to train a GMMHMM model. 

`gmmhmm_model = hmmlearn.hmm.GMMHMM(n_components=2, n_mix=3, covariance_type="full", algorithm="viterbi", init_params="stcmw", params="stcmw", n_iter=20, tol=1e-4)`

```
X = numpy.array(X).reshape(-1, 1)
gmmhmm_model.fit(X, length=[X.shape[0]])
```

Then, I use predict function to get a sequence of states given test data Y which is a set of positive floating-point numbers like X. 

```
Y = numpy.array(Y).reshape(-1, 1)
seq = gmmhmm_model.predict(Y, lengths=[Y.shape[0]])
```

I will get a sequence of 0s and 1s which correspond to the two HMM states I defined. But my question is how do you interpret these states as you don't use labels when training. Can I say, state_0 always represents small numbers and vice versa?

issue_title: Some servers don't understand quoted boundaries in multipart documents
issue_body:
## Long story short

After a prolonged debugging session I discovered that the usage of quotes in the boundary of a multipart document caused my request to be rejected by the server since it didn't support this.
Preventing the quotation marks from being added in the code makes this work.

The server in question is using nginx, but I don't know which version yet (asked their support).

## Specification

https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-3.1.1.5
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#appendix-D
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7230#section-3.2.6

    Content-Type = media-type

    media-type = type "/" subtype *( OWS ";" OWS parameter )
    parameter = token "=" ( token / quoted-string )

    token          = 1*tchar
    quoted-string  = DQUOTE *( qdtext / quoted-pair ) DQUOTE
    qdtext         = HTAB / SP /%x21 / %x23-5B / %x5D-7E / obs-text
    obs-text       = %x80-FF
    quoted-pair    = "\" ( HTAB / SP / VCHAR / obs-text )
    tchar          = "!" / "#" / "$" / "%" / "&" / "'" / "*"
                       / "+" / "-" / "." / "^" / "_" / "`" / "|" / "~"
                       / DIGIT / ALPHA
                       ; any VCHAR, except delimiters

## Expected behaviour

Specify the multipart boundary without quotes, unless they are required.

## Actual behaviour

Multipart boundary is always quoted.

## Steps to reproduce

Since I don't know which nginx version is affected, the only reliable way to reproduce right now is by filing a request at [share-online's upload API](http://www.share-online.biz/uploadapi).

A sample script is provided below (fill in your data):

<details>

```py
import asyncio
import pathlib

import aiohttp

SESSION_URL = "http://www.share-online.biz/upv3_session.php"
USER = ""
PASS = ""
path = pathlib.Path(".gitignore")


async def async_main():
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        data = {'username': USER, 'password': PASS}
        async with session.post(SESSION_URL, data=data) as resp:
            text = await resp.text()
        session_name, session_endpoint = text.split(";")
        session_endpoint = f"http://{session_endpoint}"

        file_size = path.stat().st_size
        with path.open('rb') as fp:
            data = aiohttp.FormData()
            data.add_field('username', USER)
            data.add_field('password', PASS)
            data.add_field('upload_session', session_name)
            data.add_field('chunk_no', "1")
            data.add_field('chunk_number', "1")
            data.add_field('filesize', str(file_size))
            data.add_field('fn', fp, filename=path.name, content_type="application/octet-steam")
            data.add_field('finalize', "1")

            print(f"Starting upload of {path.name}; size: {file_size}")
            async with session.post(session_endpoint, data=data) as resp:
                # print(f"response headers: {resp.headers}")
                resp.raise_for_status()
                text = await resp.text()
                print(f"reply: {text}")


def main():
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    task = asyncio.ensure_future(async_main())
    try:
        loop.run_until_complete(task)
    finally:
        loop.close()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

</details>

With this script, the upload will fail with `*** EXCEPTION session creation/reuse failed - 11-21-2017, 3:13 pm ***`.

Removing the quotes at https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/439c732f5bf29a83779dce6da4a55e7efd433cf8/aiohttp/multipart.py#L640 makes the request work.

## Your environment

Arch Linux, Python 3.6, aiohttp 2.3.3 (client)

issue_title: make_mocked_coro should be enchanced
issue_body:
## Long story short

I'm trying to write test for custom http service client.
My custom client uses `aiohttp.ClientSession`.
For each request my custom client checks if the same request is already waiting for response and wait for result instead of creating new request. 

Here is fragment of my test:
```
response = mock.Mock(json=make_mocked_coro(expected_json_data))
with mock.patch.object(my_client.session, 'get', new=make_mocked_coro(response)):
    tasks = [
        my_client.get_by('query'),  # task1
        my_client.get_by('query'),  # task2
        my_client.get_by('query')   # task3
    ]
    results, pending = await asyncio.wait(tasks)
    my_client.session.get.assert_called_once_with(...)
```
## Expected behaviour

I've expected to see switches between task1, task2, task3 when `ClientSession.get` or `response.json` called(+awaited) while debugging.

## Actual behaviour

task1 was executed without switches on `ClientSession.get` or `response.json` call(+awaiting).
So task2 and task3 didn't wait for task1 to be finished and just get cached result from task1.

## Fix

To achieve expected behaviour I have added `yield from asyncio.sleep(0)` to `mock_coro`(inside `make_mocked_coro`).

Is my fix correct? I will make PR if so.

I implement a simple ORM framework with some functions to process SQL.Some related codes in orm.py(just ignore the chinese annotation) as follows. The update or findAll method in class Model works well and really give the results correctly but every time after I running the test_method,it gives the exception.

    @asyncio.coroutine
    def create_pool(loop, **kw):		# 引入关键字后不用显示import asyncio了
        # 该函数用于创建连接池
        global __pool  # 全局变量用于保存连接池
        __pool = yield from aiomysql.create_pool(
            host=kw.get('host', 'localhost'),  # 默认定义host名字为localhost
            port=kw.get('port', 3306),		# 默认定义mysql的默认端口是3306
            user=kw['user'],				# user是通过关键字参数传进来的
            password=kw['password'],		# 密码也是通过关键字参数传进来的
            db=kw['db'],					# 数据库的名字
            charset=kw.get('charset', 'utf8'),  # 默认数据库字符集是utf8
            autocommit=kw.get('autocommit', True),  # 默认自动提交事务
            maxsize=kw.get('maxsize', 10),		# 连接池最多同时处理10个请求
            minsize=kw.get('minsize', 1),		# 连接池最少1个请求
            loop=loop		# 传递消息循环对象loop用于异步执行
        )
    @asyncio.coroutine
    def execute(sql, args, autocommit=True):
        # execute方法只返回结果数，不返回结果集,用于insert,update这些SQL语句
        log(sql)
        with (yield from __pool) as conn:
            if not autocommit:
                yield from conn.begin()
            try:
                cur = yield from conn.cursor()
                # 执行sql语句，同时替换占位符
                # pdb.set_trace()
                yield from cur.execute(sql.replace('?', '%s'), args)
                affected = cur.rowcount     # 返回受影响的行数
                yield from cur.close()       # 关闭游标
                if not autocommit:
                    yield from conn.commit()
            except BaseException as e:
                if not autocommit:
                    yield from conn.rollback()
                raise e  # raise不带参数，则把此处的错误往上抛;为了方便理解还是建议加e吧
            return affected


    class Model(dict, metaclass=ModelMetaclass):
        # 继承dict是为了使用方便，例如对象实例user['id']即可轻松通过UserModel去数据库获取到id
        # 元类自然是为了封装我们之前写的具体的SQL处理函数，从数据库获取数据
    
        def __init__(self, **kw):
            # 调用dict的父类__init__方法用于创建Model,super(类名，类对象)
            super(Model, self).__init__(**kw)
    
        def __getattr__(self, key):
            # 调用不存在的属性时返回一些内容
            try:
                return self[key]  # 如果存在则正常返回
            except KeyError:
                raise AttributeError(
                    r"'Model' object has no attribute '%s'" % key)		# r表示不转义
    
        def __setattr__(self, key, value):
            # 设定Model里面的key-value对象，这里value允许为None
            self[key] = value
    
        def getValue(self, key):
            # 获取某个具体的值，肯定存在的情况下使用该函数,否则会使用__getattr()__
            # 获取实例的key，None是默认值，getattr方法使用可以参考http://kaimingwan.com/post/python/pythonzhong-de-nei-zhi-han-shu-getattr-yu-fan-she
            return getattr(self, key, None)
    
        def getValueOrDefault(self, key):
            # 这个方法当value为None的时候能够返回默认值
            value = getattr(self, key, None)
            if value is None:		# 不存在这样的值则直接返回
                # self.__mapping__在metaclass中，用于保存不同实例属性在Model基类中的映射关系
                field = self.__mappings__[key]
                if field.default is not None:  # 如果实例的域存在默认值，则使用默认值
                    # field.default是callable的话则直接调用
                    value = field.default() if callable(field.default) else field.default
                    logging.debug('using default value for %s:%s' %
                                  (key, str(value)))
                    setattr(self, key, value)
            return value
    
    
    # --------------------------每个Model类的子类实例应该具备的执行SQL的方法比如save------
        @classmethod    # 类方法
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def findAll(cls, where=None, args=None, **kw):
            sql = [cls.__select__]  # 获取默认的select语句
            if where:   # 如果有where语句，则修改sql变量
                # 这里不用协程，是因为不需要等待数据返回
                sql.append('where')  # sql里面加上where关键字
                sql.append(where)   # 这里的where实际上是colName='xxx'这样的条件表达式
            if args is None:    # 什么参数?
                args = []
    
            orderBy = kw.get('orderBy', None)    # 从kw中查看是否有orderBy属性
            if orderBy:
                sql.append('order by')
                sql.append(orderBy)
    
            limit = kw.get('limit', None)    # mysql中可以使用limit关键字
            if limit is not None:
                sql.append('limit')
                if isinstance(limit, int):   # 如果是int类型则增加占位符
                    sql.append('?')
                    args.append(limit)
                elif isinstance(limit, tuple) and len(limit) == 2:   # limit可以取2个参数，表示一个范围
                    sql.append('?,?')
                    args.extend(limit)
                else:       # 其他情况自然是语法问题
                    raise ValueError('Invalid limit value: %s' % str(limit))
                # 在原来默认SQL语句后面再添加语句，要加个空格
    
            rs = yield from select(' '.join(sql), args)
            return [cls(**r) for r in rs]   # 返回结果，结果是list对象，里面的元素是dict类型的
    
        @classmethod
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def findNumber(cls, selectField, where=None, args=None):
            # 获取行数
            # 这里的 _num_ 什么意思？别名？ 我估计是mysql里面一个记录实时查询结果条数的变量
            sql = ['select %s _num_ from `%s`' % (selectField, cls.__table__)]
            # pdb.set_trace()
            if where:
                sql.append('where')
                sql.append(where)   # 这里不加空格？
            rs = yield from select(' '.join(sql), args, 1)  # size = 1
            if len(rs) == 0:  # 结果集为0的情况
                    return None
            return rs[0]['_num_']   # 有结果则rs这个list中第一个词典元素_num_这个key的value值
    
        @classmethod
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def find_by_key(cls, pk):
            # 根据主键查找
            # pk是dict对象
            rs = yield from select('%s where `%s`=?' % (cls.__select__, cls.__primary_key__), [pk], 1)
            if len(rs) == 0:
                return None
            return cls(**rs[0])
    
        # 这个是实例方法
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def save(self):
            # arg是保存所有Model实例属性和主键的list,使用getValueOrDefault方法的好处是保存默认值
            # 将自己的fields保存进去
            args = list(map(self.getValueOrDefault, self.__fields__))
            args.append(self.getValueOrDefault(self.__primary_key__))
            # pdb.set_trace()
            rows = yield from execute(self.__insert__, args)  # 使用默认插入函数
            if rows != 1:
                # 插入失败就是rows!=1
                logging.warn(
                    'failed to insert record: affected rows: %s' % rows)
    
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def update(self):
            # 这里使用getValue说明只能更新那些已经存在的值，因此不能使用getValueOrDefault方法
            args = list(map(self.getValue, self.__fields__))
            args.append(self.getValue(self.__primary_key__))
            # pdb.set_trace()
            rows = yield from execute(self.__update__, args)    # args是属性的list
            if rows != 1:
                logging.warn(
                    'failed to update by primary key: affected rows: %s' % rows)
    
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def remove(self):
            args = [self.getValue(self.__primary_key__)]
            # pdb.set_trace()
            rows = yield from execute(self.__delete__, args)
            if rows != 1:
                logging.warn(
                    'failed to remove by primary key: affected rows: %s' % rows)

issue_title: Mac Installation / Live Audio Example
issue_body:
Hi,

I recently got PS with GStreamer working on Mac High Sierra. It was a bit convoluted and I'd like to share my results in case it helps other users. Let me know if you think there's an appropriate place to include the following info…

## Install PocketSphinx

### Attempts
1. FAILED: ~~`brew install cmu-pocketsphinx`~~ Very old version (0.8) which doesn't match docs
2. Found tap to install HEAD on [GH](https://github.com/watsonbox/homebrew-cmu-sphinx)

## GStreamer Integration
The PS docs say that leveraging GStream makes continuous mode easier, however, we couldn't get it working.
1. Installed via `brew install gstreamer gst-plugins-base gst-plugins-good` (last one was for testing)
2. Build gst-pocketsphinx plugin
  - ~~FAILED: Reinstall PS to build the plugin~~; couldn't find gstreamer; posted question on both the [homebrew](https://discourse.brew.sh/t/pkg-config-does-not-respect-pkg-config-path/895) and [PS](https://sourceforge.net/p/cmusphinx/discussion/help/thread/fd2c2091/) MLs
  - Cloned and built PS from scratch following the last script from [this SO thread](https://stackoverflow.com/q/27485820/424245)
    - Manually copied the plugin (`*.so` file) to `/usr/local/lib/gstreamer-1.0`; NB: aliasing did not work, maybe a symlink would have
3. Test
  - Recognize sound file: `gst-launch-1.0  filesrc  location=~/Documents/Reference/Foreign\ Language/Speak\ From\ Day\ One/audio_book/00_about.mp3  !  mpegaudioparse   ! avdec_mp3 ! audioconvert ! audioresample ! pocketsphinx ! fdsink fd=1`
  - Recognize live sound
    - Tried ~~`gst-launch-1.0 autoaudiosrc ! audioconvert ! audioresample ! pocketsphinx name=asr ! fakesink`~~ but thought it wasn't working because no output; realized that `fakesink` *means* throw away output!
    - Tried `gst-launch-1.0 autoaudiosrc ! audioconvert ! audioresample ! pocketsphinx name=asr ! fdsink fd=1`, which returned (poorly) recognized text, but warned that `autoaudiosrc0-actual-src-osxaudi: Can't record audio fast enough`.
    - Added `queue` per [the gst ML](http://gstreamer-devel.966125.n4.nabble.com/Can-t-record-audio-fast-enough-issue-On-iOS-device-tp4670440.html) to get: `gst-launch-1.0 autoaudiosrc ! queue ! audioconvert ! audioresample ! pocketsphinx name=asr ! fdsink fd=1`. This thread also seemed to have [some interesting ideas]((http://gstreamer-devel.966125.n4.nabble.com/Can-t-record-audio-fast-enough-tp967670p967671.html)) about how to address this.
4. Try python bindings
  - `brew install `
  - ~~Workaround: `export PYTHONPATH=$PYTHONPATH:/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages` (per [this GH issue](https://github.com/Homebrew/legacy-homebrew/issues/46518))~~
  - Link Homebrew python module target to python3: `echo "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages" > /usr/local/Cellar/python/3.6.5/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/Homebrew.pth` per [this SO thread](https://stackoverflow.com/a/12311321/424245)

## Usage Example - Live Audio
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python

# Adapted from:
#   - https://github.com/GStreamer/gst-python/blob/master/examples/helloworld.py
#   - With parts from https://cmusphinx.github.io/wiki/gstreamer/

import sys
import signal

import gi
gi.require_version('Gst', '1.0')
from gi.repository import GObject, Gst

#For IPC
import socket

def on_element_message(bus, message, loop):
    """Receive element messages from the bus."""
    msgtype = message.get_structure().get_name()
    if msgtype != 'pocketsphinx':
        return

    if message.get_structure().get_value('final'):
        final_result(message.get_structure().get_value('hypothesis'),
                     message.get_structure().get_value('confidence'))

def final_result(hyp, confidence):
    #Debug - reference https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/inputoutput.html#fancier-output-formatting
    #print('Final: {}; confidence: {}'.format(hyp, confidence))
    send_command(hyp)

def send_command(command):
    # Create a TCP/IP socket
    sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)

    # Connect the socket to the port where the server is listening
    server_address = ('localhost', 9999)
    #print >>sys.stderr, 'connecting to %s port %s' % server_address
    sock.connect(server_address)

    try:
        
        # Send data
        #print >>sys.stderr, 'sending "%s"' % command
        sock.sendall(command.encode()) #.encode per https://stackoverflow.com/a/37376668/424245
        
        # Look for the response
        amount_received = 0
        amount_expected = len(command)
        
        while amount_received < amount_expected:
            data = sock.recv(16)
            amount_received += len(data)
            #print >>sys.stderr, 'received "%s"' % data

    finally:
        #print >>sys.stderr, 'closing socket'
        sock.close()

def main(args):
    # Before using Python threads, or libraries using threads (GStreamer for example), you have to call GObject.threads_init(). Contrary to the naming, this function isn't provided by gobject but initializes thread support in PyGObject (it was called gobject.threads_init() in pygtk). Think of it as gi.threads_init().
    # Since PyGObject 3.10.2, calling GObject.threads_init() this is no longer needed.
    # Per https://wiki.gnome.org/Projects/PyGObject/Threading
    GObject.threads_init()
    Gst.init(None) # Cleanup via gst-deinit is normally not needed in an application as the resources will automatically be freed when the program terminates https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/data/doc/gstreamer/head/gstreamer/html/gstreamer-Gst.html#gst-deinit

    # Doc: https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/data/doc/gstreamer/head/gstreamer/html/gstreamer-GstParse.html#gst-parse-launch
    pipeline = Gst.parse_launch('autoaudiosrc ! queue ! audioconvert !' +
                            ' audioresample ! pocketsphinx name=asr !' +
                            ' fakesink') # to debug, use "fdsink fd=1" for last arg to write to stdout
    if not pipeline:
        sys.stderr.write("Failed to create pipeline\n")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    pocketsphinx = pipeline.get_by_name('asr')
    #From https://raspberrypi.stackexchange.com/a/56818
    pocketsphinx.set_property('kws', '/Users/sean/Documents/Reference/Smalltalk/Repositories/seandenigris/DynaChef/asr/experiments/continuous/command.list')

    # create and event loop and feed gstreamer bus mesages to it
    loop = GObject.MainLoop.new(None, False)
    
    bus = pipeline.get_bus()
    bus.add_signal_watch()
    bus.connect ('message::element', on_element_message, loop)
    
    # start play back and listed to events
    pipeline.set_state(Gst.State.PLAYING)
    try:
        loop.run()
    except:
        pass

    # cleanup
    print('Cleaning up...')
    pipeline.set_state(Gst.State.NULL)

    # Block until pipeline changes state (per http://gstreamer-devel.966125.n4.nabble.com/Gstreamer-critical-on-pipeline-set-state-Gst-State-NULL-tp4678899p4678912.html), but the docs say changes to null are never async and suggest an alternate check (https://lazka.github.io/pgi-docs/Gst-1.0/classes/Element.html#Gst.Element.set_state)
    pipeline.get_state(Gst.CLOCK_TIME_NONE)
    loop.quit()
    print('Done!')

    return 0

if __name__ == '__main__':
    sys.exit(main(sys.argv))
```

issue_title: True read-only cells
issue_body:
nbgrader currently has pseudo "read-only" cells where they are "read-only" in the sense that their contents get saved to the nbgrader database, and then overwritten during grading if they have been changed by the student. I have reports that this is confusing, and requests for true read-only cells where the cell actually cannot be edited at all in the live notebook.

issue_title: Divide by zero in np.power
issue_body:
I've  been running into divide by zero issues when using `anp.power` with inputs equaling zero. This issue seems similar to #93. The [line](https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/autograd/numpy/numpy_grads.py#L77) in question seems to be:
```
anp.power.defgrad(lambda ans, x, y : unbroadcast(ans, x, lambda g :\
    g * y * x ** (anp.where(y, y - 1, 1.))))
```

If this is a bug, would the following modification suffice?
```
anp.power.defgrad(lambda ans, x, y : unbroadcast(ans, x, lambda g :\
    g * y * x ** (anp.where(anp.logical_and(y, x),  y - 1, 1.))))
```
If additional numerical stability is needed, `anp.logical_and(y, x)` could be swapped out for `anp.logical_and(y, anp.greater(anp.abs(x), const)` where `const` is some small positive constant, e.g., `10.0*anp.finfo(x.dtype).eps`.

issue_title: API: return structured zone files, or error message where appropriate
issue_body: There are a few cases where we return a raw zone file as part of an aggregate JSON object, such as name whois info.  This should not be allowed--either a well-formed zone file may be returned (i.e. one that is RFC-compliant), or an error message should be returned.  The reason is that non-compliant zone files may be arbitrary byte strings, which is not compatible with JSON.

issue_title: Failing Tests pass if fixtures use event loop
issue_body:
This came up when I was writing tests for some code that used `asyncpg`. 

Essentially, if a pytest fixture uses the `@pytest.mark.asyncio` decorator or just directly calls the event loop from inside a synchronous function, the async tests which require that fixture will pass regardless of their content.

I've put together a minimal example here https://github.com/cshenton/pytest-asyncio-bug that only uses the standard library and `pytest`.

There are async tests, each of which awaits `asyncio.sleep()` then calls `assert False`. They all depend on a fixture which also calls `asyncio.sleep()`, in different ways, and they all pass on my machine.

issue_title: [MacOS, Linux] NuGet writes the NuGet.Config in ~/.config folder, but dotnet restore reads from ~/.nuget
issue_body:
When using NuGet to add a private feed, `NuGet Sources Add` writes in `~/.config/NuGet/NuGet.Config` file. But when using `dotnet restore` the `~/.nuget/NuGet/NuGet.Config` file is used to read the `packageSources` and `packageSourceCredentials`. 

This issue forces users to manually copy and paste all necessary information instead of having that generated automatically by the NuGet CLI. 

NuGet repo issue: https://github.com/NuGet/Home/issues/4413

issue_title: Recognize timezones e.g "US/Eastern"
issue_body: The colloquial "EST", "EDT", "PST", etc are technically ambiguous, but "US/Eastern", "US/Pacific", etc should be recognized by the parser.

issue_title: Non Local timezones not supported by parse() with ignoretz=False
issue_body:
Note in the examples below that only the PST date gets a tzinfo when ignoretz=False is used. And it is tzlocal(). (I am in the PST timezone). For CST and EST, ignoretz=False still produces a naive datetime.
```
>>> parse("1996.07.10 AD at 15:08:56 PST", ignoretz=True)
datetime.datetime(1996, 7, 10, 15, 8, 56)
>>> 
>>> parse("1996.07.10 AD at 15:08:56 PST", ignoretz=False)
datetime.datetime(1996, 7, 10, 15, 8, 56, tzinfo=tzlocal())
>>> 
>>> parse("1996.07.10 AD at 15:08:56 CST", ignoretz=False)
datetime.datetime(1996, 7, 10, 15, 8, 56)
>>> 
>>> parse("1996.07.10 AD at 15:08:56 EST", ignoretz=False)
datetime.datetime(1996, 7, 10, 15, 8, 56)
```

issue_title: Create markers with custom icons
issue_body:
The one thing barring me from using Folium is the lack of support for markers using custom icons, through the use of user-provided .png files. This functionality is offered with Leaflet - http://leafletjs.com/examples/custom-icons.html

According to the text, "Marker icons in Leaflet are defined by L.Icon objects, which are passed as an option when creating markers."

For me personally, the option of including a shadow icon, as offered by Leaflet, is unnecessary, but I don't want to speak on behalf of everyone who desires this functionality.

I don't think I have the coding chops to actually add this functionality on my own. I'm eager to learn, but for the time being, if someone's willing to take this on, it would be enormously appreciated.

# Setup
* Latest powerline installed.
* zsh or bash set up with default theme for powerline
* Tmux is configured with vi copy mode bindings: `set-window-option -g mode-keys vi`
*  A shell runs inside any of the latest year's tmux versions.

# How to trigger problem
First you type some shell command, e.g.

```shell
 09:43:03  INSERT  erikw  ~  echo test msg
```

Then you want to copy copy this command, and to do that with tmux copy mode. So you enter copy mode with `prefix-]` and go up to the end of the command with the intention to step backwards to the "e" in echo and then press enter to make a copy.


Put cursor here
```shell
 09:43:03  INSERT  erikw  ~  echo test msg
                                                ^
```

then using normal vi command to step one word back at the time, press `b`, which takes you to the beginning of the previous word, like


```shell
 09:43:03  INSERT  erikw  ~  echo test msg
                                         ^
```

all good. As a vi user I know now that I only have to press `b` one more time to end up at the "e" of echo, and then I'm done with my selection and can copy the command.

Problem is here!

The expected outcome of pressing `b` now is:

```shell
 09:43:03  INSERT  erikw  ~  echo test msg
                                    ^
```

However with powerline installed in zsh or bash, this is the actual outcome here:

```shell
 09:43:03  INSERT  erikw  ~  echo test msg
^
```

It steps all the way back to the beginning of the line. Terrible, now I have to step forward using forward search, or estimate how many characters to jump forward etc.


Without powerline in zsh or bash, tmux `b` in copy mode works as expected, no problem. I only have this with powerline theme applied. I tried changing the theme to add some extra spaces at the trailing `>` at the end of the shell status line, but that did not help.

There is something with powerline that make tmux text selection consider the part after the last `> ` and the first character of the command to be one long contiguous word. It looks visually as there is a space between `> echo` but text selection considers it a whole word for some reason.


Why is this worth fixing? Well because copying old commands is probably the most common use case for tmux and copy mode, so this is annoying every time one try to do that. :)

Anyone have ideas on how we can make the status line not be considered a long word? :)

issue_title: Body parameters renamed "in"
Pyserial struggles communicating with arduino
issue_body:
Hi,

I don't know if it's the right, or best place to ask for some help, but here it is:
I am working on a laser projector project. The kind that you use in music shows. This project is made of two parts, a python program running on a computer and featuring a GUI, and an arduino part that drives the projector hardware.
The PC part opens an image file, computes position for each of its pixels, format it in json style (e.g. {"ID":0,"X":300,"mode":1}) then streams it to the arduino.
The arduino parses this json string, get var/value pair, populates a buffer with it, then when done asks for a new json string. For debugging purpose it can also send informations, that are formatted the same way.
The python program has a main loop, that calls the function ones after others: GUI update, sending json serial data, reading serial data.
Writing serial data is simple: it uses the write() method, then sets a flag so that the next writing step will escape, unless the arduino has asked.
For reading, readline() was not good because it was blocking. So instead on each iteration it tests for available data, read() it and add it to a string. If the received byte is an end of line '\n', then it sets a flag and a parser will read the data. Possibly the next write iteration will be made, and the cycle starts again.

Now, here is the problem I have: as I have a huge amount of data to transfer as fast as possible (images are several millions of pixels), I try to limit communication as much as possible. So the arduino sends the minimum, and once I was sure it was working well, I've started to suppress the sending of debugging information from the arduino. Each message that is not sent shows an improve of global speed, until a point where there's just one information that comes from the arduino to the PC. Unfortunately at this point, it's like if the python program can't handle the data speed, and I don't understand why.
With two strings sent by the arduino for each coordinate received (one that gives the board state, and the other for asking next json string), it's about 22 seconds to send 2500 coordinates, including print() of each of these strings. Keeping just the one I need, it hardly can handle 2 to 5 sendings a second.

Here are the settings I'm using: 
baudrate = 115200
timeout = 0

I hope someone will be able to explain me what happens, or tell me what I'm doing wrong. I can, if needed, give some code.

issue_title: Setting the timeout to 0 delivers an immediate timeout on Linux, but is ignored on Windows
issue_body:
I noticed by accident that the behaviour of
```
(set-option :TIMEOUT 0)
```
differs on Linux and Windows Z3 binaries. On Windows, this becomes an infinite timeout, whereas on Linux it's really a zero timeout (every query is immediately cancelled). From a quick guess at the code, I think the Windows version behaves the way it does is because CreateTimerQueueTimer() is called with a period of zero, which means that it's a one-shot timer, and the first callback is ignored.

I noticed this because Boogie sets the timeout to zero by default if you don't specify one, and evidently they are expecting it to be infinite.

issue_title: objective values and smt-lib compliance
issue_body:
This is prompted by #1062 actually. With the most recent commit of @wintersteiger I am now explicitly putting

 `    (set-option :smtlib2_compliant true)`

in my scripts, as otherwise the `echo` doesn't work as expected. I'm OK with this actually, since I'd like to keep everything SMT-lib compliant.

However, I hit an issue. Consider this input:

```
; (set-option :smtlib2_compliant true)
(set-option :print-success false)
(set-logic ALL)
(declare-fun s0 () Real)
(assert (> s0 0.0))
(maximize (- 1.0 s0))
(check-sat)
```

Z3 nicely responds:

```
sat
(objectives
 ((- 1.0 s0) (+ (to_real 1) (* (to_real (- 1)) epsilon)))
)
```
But if I uncomment the very first line to make it smt-lib compliant, I get:

```
success
sat
(objectives
 ((- 1.0 s0)(error "line 7 column 10: Sort mismatch at argument #2 for function (declare-fun * (Int Int) Int) supplied sort is Real")
```

Perhaps this is understandable, as I guess objective-values/optimization etc. is not strictly smt-lib compliant.

_But you see my dilemma now: If I want smt-lib compliance, then I don't get optimization. If I don't set smt-lib compliance, then `echo` doesn't work._

Is there a happy medium we can create? Ideally, I'd like to keep the smtlib-compilant flag true, but I definitely don't want to lose optimization either.

Perhaps allow for optimization/epsilon etc. even in the smt-lib mode, via a specific set-option:

`(set-option :support_optimization true)`

If that's not feasible, what would you recommend as a potential solution?

issue_title: API request: error code for cancellation
issue_body:
Hi,

When using an error handler we need to differentiate between exception arising from cancellations, and exceptions arising from actual errors.
From C API the only way we could do that is by comparing the string returned by Z3_get_error_code to "canceled".
This is a bit scary, since the string "canceled" seems to be hardcoded in quite a few places inside Z3.

Would it be possible to add cancellation to Z3_error_code instead?

issue_title: Adding YellowFin to the lasagne/optimizers
issue_body:
YellowFin is a novel method for tuning momentum hyperparameters (learning rate & momentum). It blends the nice sides of momentum SGD (convergence properties) and Adam and similar (automatic tuning of the hyperparameters, in contrast to hand-tuning them).

The original paper (https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03471) shows some nice convergence speed-ups (2.5x on CIFAR against Adam, for example). I would like to start implementing it as soon as #843 will be resolved.

issue_title: feature request: synchrosqueezed wavelet transform
issue_body:
Original feature request by @gabsee at https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/6888:

Is anyone savvy enough to add the new **Wavelet Synchrosqueezed Transform (wsst)** (including inverse **iwsst**) to SciPy?

WSST has recently been implemented in MATLAB (R2016a): https://au.mathworks.com/help/wavelet/ref/wsst.html

The method is based on these two papers:
1. [I. Daubechies, I., J. Lu, and H. T. Wu. "Synchrosqueezed Wavelet Transforms: an Empricial Mode Decomposition-like Tool", Applied and Computational Harmonic Analysis. Vol. 30(2), pp. 243–261.](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1063520310001016)
2. [Thakur, G., E. Brevdo, N. S. Fučkar, and H. T. Wu. "The Synchrosqueezing algorithm for time-varying spectral analysis: robustness properties and new paleoclimate applications." Signal Processing. Vol. 93, pp. 1079–1094.](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1105.0010)

The second one contains the algorithm and is available from arxiv.

I have used this method a few times for research projects and it is fantastic! However, I am new to Python, so am unable to implement this (just yet). Availability of wsst/iwsst in SciPy would convince me to give up Matlab for good.

Many thanks for considering :)

issue_title: As a user, I can automatically update my distribution
issue_labels: [{'id': 294019786, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/PulpQE/pulp-smash/labels/Issue%20Type:%20Test%20Case', 'name': 'Issue Type: Test Case', 'color': 'bfd4f2', 'default': False}, {'id': 639323840, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/PulpQE/pulp-smash/labels/Pulp%20Version:%203', 'name': 'Pulp Version: 3', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
This feature was added without an issue, but it is related to
https://pulp.plan.io/issues/3295

It is documented by this issue:
https://pulp.plan.io/issues/3392

A Distribution has 2 fields that specify auto-distribution:

- publisher
- repository

Either both of these are set or neither are set. I think this is already tested, but not sure. Assuming they are both set, whenever the Distribution.publisher creates a new Publication of the Distribution.repository, this Distribution will automatically be updated to the new Publication.

Simple case
1. Create a Distribution with publisher and repository attrs.
2. Publish using the Publisher and Repository
3. Make sure Distribution points to new Publication.

Overwrite case
1. Create a Distribution with publisher and repository attrs.
2. Associate the Distribution to a Publication
2. Publish using the Publisher and Repository
3. Make sure Distribution points to new Publication.

Unset case
(continued from either)
1. Update the Distribution, removing publisher and repository 
2. Publish the repo
3. Make the Distribution does not point to the new Publication.

issue_title: Modify Pulp Smash for the Pulp 3 transition to RQ
issue_body:
Pulp 3 is planning to switch from tasking using Celery to tasking using RQ.  This means that Pulp will no longer require a message broker like Qpid or RabbitMQ.

This won't require too much modification to Pulp Smash, but the main ones I can think of are:

* There's no need to ask whether the user is using Qpid or RabbitMQ, when using ```pulp-smash settings create``` with Pulp 3

* There's no need for the ```amqp_broker``` section in the ```settings.json```

* The status API page will change.

There is a PR open to make these changes (https://github.com/PulpQE/pulp-smash/pull/960), and this issue might not necessarily be an exhaustive list.

│ pyyaml                     │ 3.13      │ <4                       │ 36333    │
╞══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╡
│ pyyaml before 4 uses ``yaml.load`` which has been assigned CVE-2017-18342.   │
╘══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╛
```

There doesn't appear to be a non-pre-release version of PyYAML on PyPI of version 4.

There are ways for me to work around this (add ignore, create and maintain requirements alongside setup.py, etc.) but I thought it might be helpful for anyone else that runs into this. Curious what your thoughts are too around some way to check only non-dev dependencies. I really like checking whatever pip has installed, but that will always catch everything installed.

issue_title: encoding not passed to stream
issue_body:
on line 90 of book.py
file_stream = _convert_content_to_stream(file_content, self._file_type)

does not pass the encoding to use so when decode is used later it throws an decoding exception 

it should maybe use the **keywords and pass it to the function like so 
file_stream = _convert_content_to_stream(file_content, self._file_type, **keywords)

to maybe something like the below. currently the file_content.decode is set to static 'utf-8'
def _convert_content_to_stream(file_content, file_type, **keywords):
    io = manager.get_io(file_type)
    encoding = keywords.get('encoding', 'utf-8')
    if PY2:
        io.write(file_content)
    else:
        if (isinstance(io, StringIO) and isinstance(file_content, bytes)):
            content = file_content.decode(encoding)

issue_title: Reestructurar ficheros de código en code, readers, writers, helpers
issue_body:
Reemplazar:
```
pydatajson/
    pydatajson.py
    xlsx_to_json.py
```
por
```
pydatajson/
    core.py
    readers.py
    writers.py
    helpers.py
```
según correspondan:
- core: clase DataJson, con métodos de validación de metadatos y generación de reportes/informes
- readers: todos los métodos para leer archivos/representaciones externas (JSONs genéricos, catálogos, XLSXs de catálogos, worksheet genéricas) y convertirlas a representaciones internas (tablas, diccionarios)
- writers: todos los métodos para escribir representaciones internas a archivos/representaciones externas (JSONs, catálogos, tablas en CSV y XLSX)
- helpers: Otros métodos de soporte a core, que no son readers ni writers.

issue_title: Tokenisers for pre-trained word vectors for scriptio continua
issue_body:
Follow-up to https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText/issues/378 as I can't re-open it.

I couldn’t find information on which tokenisers were used for training the published word vectors. I’d like to align tokenisers when using these word vectors for my models.

The [paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1607.04606.pdf) describes pre-processing for languages all of which use spaces to divide words (section 4.4); and [readWord in Dictionary](https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText/blob/master/src/dictionary.cc#L201) expects words to be separated by space characters. Looks like tokenisation process here is = separating the sentence by space characters.

Where can I find information on how tokenisation was performed for languages that don't use spaces to separate words (Chinese, Japanese, Thai)?

issue_title: Isn't clear how to use the testing backend
issue_body:
So I've been using anymail for a while to send emails but now I've started work on tracking emails and I'm running into some issues around testing. The issue at the moment is that I've got lots of places in the code that read:

```
msg = self.generate_email()
msg.send()

email_sent = self.create_sent_object(email_id=msg.message_id)
```

Where generate_email is creating an EmailMultiAlternatives object. This is failing in tests because msg.message_id isn't working in tests. Presumably this is because Django is switching the email backend out on tests so they don't actually get sent. Based off what I've seen in this packages tests I've tried:

```
@override_settings(EMAIL_BACKEND='anymail.backends.test.TestBackend', ANYMAIL_TEST_SAMPLE_SETTING='sample',)
```

But that doesn't seem to be helping at all, also I have no idea what ANYMAIL_TEST_SAMPLE_SETTING is doing so 'sample' might be wrong. I can't seem to find anything in the docs covering what to do in this situation. Happy to write it up into the docs once I've got it working as it might be useful for other people.

issue_title: Automatically handle ACMEv1 vs. ACMEv2 issuance
issue_body:
Part of #5367.


From https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/5554#issuecomment-364621848: 

For the methods that try and handle the differences, we first add a method like `request_challenges` or `request_authorizations` that takes either a CSR or list of domains and returns a list of authorizations. A CSR is only necessary if `new_order` takes a CSR. For ACMEv1, it takes the domains (possibly after extracting them from the CSR) and calls `request_domain_challenges` for each domain and returns a list of obtained authzs. For ACMEv2, this calls `new_order` with either the CSR/domains and returns the authorizations in the Order. This order object is stored as an attribute of the class to be handled internally.

Next we add a method like `request_issuance` which takes a CSR and returns the cert and its chain. For ACMEv1, it forwards the arguments to `request_issuance` and then retries calling `fetch_chain` (see `certbot.client.obtain_certificate_from_csr`). For ACMEv2 it adds the CSR to the internal order object (if necessary) and calls `poll_order_and_request_issuance`. We then need to split the certificate and chain, probably by looking for the first line containing "END CERTIFICATE". If the `request_challenges`/`request_authorizations` method takes a CSR, we could keep this as an attribute of the class too and remove the parameter here if we really wanted.

issue_body:
I have a model structure similar to the following example:

```python
# models.py

import uuid
from django.db import models
from polymorphic.models import PolymorphicModel


class BaseObject(PolymorphicModel):
    id = models.UUIDField(primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)


class Creature(BaseObject):
    pass


class Animal(Creature):

class Pet(Animal):
    pass


class Dog(Pet):
    pass

If the inheritance is more than three levels deep, I start getting strange behaviour when querying for objects:

```python
Python 3.5.3 (default, Jan 19 2017, 14:11:04)

issue_title: Support fractional GPU usage.
issue_body:
It is common for multiple users of the same machine to share a single GPU. For example, several users may each train their own neural net on the same GPU, and as long as the aggregate memory usage does not exceed the GPU memory, then this should work out.

Some thoughts and questions.

1. What's the best way to support this in the API? For example, something like
    ```python
    @ray.remote(num_gpus=0.5)
    def f():
      ...
    ```

2. It's more or less clear what to do if the user requests less than one GPU, but what if they request 1.5 GPUs. For that to be meaningful the specification would probably have to be more specific (for example, 3/4 of 2 GPUs, or 1 full GPU and 1/2 of another GPU). Does this come up in practice? Or should we just not support this case?

3. Presumably it'd be the responsibility of the user to not actually user more than 1/2 GPU if that's what the task requested. It probably wouldn't be super easy for Ray to enforce this. If the task does use more, then at worst this should cause that task to fail (and possibly any other task that was using the same GPU). Ideally the failure would take the form of a Python exception.

issue_title: scheduler differences between wait and get ?
issue_body:
After finding some places in our flow where we don't need to block and can do some processing on intermediate results we changed our code to use wait instead of get.  In this case our cluster's machines never got a load over 10 (on a 56 core box) where with the get during the main processing the load was maxed out.

Here is some pseudo code of what we were doing in each case:

Get version:

```
results = ray.get([func.remote() for i in range(100)])

for x in results:
   Do work
```

Wait version:

```
results = [func.remote() for i in range(100)]

while True:
   ready, remaining = ray.wait(results)
   results = remaining

   for x in ready:
       data = ray.get(x)
       Do work
  
  if len(remaining) == 0
     break
```

No other code changes were made and we see significant load differences.

Also, as a test we tried changing the wait to block with num_returns=100 (in the above) and the wait version is still very low load with this change where it seems to us that the wait with that number blocking should be equivalent.

issue_title: Unintended object mutation
issue_body:
in caldav/davclient.py:
DAVClient.request lines 221-222:
        combined_headers = self.headers
        combined_headers.update(headers)
First line makes combined_headers to be a *reference* to self.headers, and therefore second line, in fact, updates self.headers with values from headers, affecting all subsequent requests.
The combined_headers should be a copy instead:
         combined_headers = dict(self.headers)
         combined_headers.update(headers)

issue_title: Error rendering Jupyter widget. Widget not found
issue_body: As discussed in https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/issues/1667#issuecomment-329029508, this message conveys the idea of something is going wrong. It is my understanding that Jupyter was trying to say "the state of this widget was not found" or maybe "the instance of the widget was not found", but not the _widget_ itself. Perhaps, the simplest solution would be to change the message to be clearer. It is more like a warning than an error.

issue_title: Move to es2015 compile target
issue_body:
It looks like even if we are supporting notebook 4.4 (released beginning of 2017), the supported browsers are current chrome/safari/firefox for that time, and all of those support es2015 modules. So I think it's fine to upgrade our typescript compile target to es2015 instead of es5.

Might as well upgrade to typescript 3.0 while we are at it.

issue_title: trio.Queue methods do not play with inspect.iscoroutinefunction
issue_body:
I've been studying trio for a while now and boy, it is a breath of fresh air -- in terms of API design, documentation and even in the clarity of design (and code) it brings to me. Thank you @njsmith  for your fantastic explanations in your blogs and in the docs.

Now, I've run into a situation where trio seemingly does not reflect the nature of the function correctly.

`python
>>> import trio, inspect
>>> q = trio.Queue(2)
>>> inspect.iscoroutinefunction(q.put)
False
>>> inspect.iscoroutinefunction(q.get)
False
`
These are `async def` functions, but it seems that the `@_core.enable_ki_protection` decorator messes with the introspectability of the code.

A couple of searches on this repo and in the docs did not throw up any information. Is this a bug?

issue_title: Enable setting PYTHONHOME to support applications that distribute Python
issue_body:
There's been a request to set PYTHONHOME for an application that distributes Python with the application.  The Python C-API also supports this: 

- https://docs.python.org/2/c-api/init.html#c.Py_SetPythonHome
- https://docs.python.org/3/c-api/init.html#c.Py_SetPythonHome

There's an open question of the route to take to set it: if we should use the C-API call `Py_SetPythonHome(arg)` or use Java's `System.loadLibrary("python")` or `System.load(filename)`.

See https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/jep-project/9TOYn3ElbFw for more details.

issue_title: Bundle TensorShapeProto with Tensor photos
issue_body:
Shape is a property of tensor types, one that no other type in the type system has. Rather than placing TensorShapeProto everywhere TypeProto appears, it would make more sense to move TensorShapeProto inside the Tensor messages.

This is a syntactic change, and should have any impact on the semantics or processing of shapes for inferencing or otherwise.
